converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Paul Jones Chapman papers
Chapman, P. J. (Paul Jones), 1900-
22-1-2102
3.2 cubic feet. 3.2 cubic feet.
1940-1992. Correspondence and research materials. Includes correspondence concerning the graduate program of the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, 1940-1983; selection of a head for the Entomology Department at Cornell University, 1965; and Rachel Carson's SILENT SPRING, 1962-1963. Also, research material concerning SILENT SPRING. Collection material in English 2077826
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
Guy E. Smith
Date completed:
March 2004
EAD encoding:
Peter Martinez, May 2004
NOTES
Related collection:Geneva Experiment Station Project. Oral History Interview with Paul Chapman, #13-6-944, trsc.3888.
SERIES LIST
Series I. Materials on Rachel Carson's Silent Spring Box 1 folders1-5
Series II. Correspondence Box 1 folders 6-15
Series III. Administrative-Miscellaneous Box 1 folders 16-18
Series IV. National Science Foundation Grants Box 1 folders 19-23
Series V. Annual Review Articles Box 1 folders 24-29
Series VI. Articles, Papers, etc. Box 1 folders 30-49 Box 2 folders 1-16
Series VII. Station History Publication Box 2 folders 17-30
Series VIII. Photo Album- "P. J. Chapman, The First 68 Years" Box 3
Series IX. Publications Box 4 folders 1-3; 15
Series X. Correspondence Box 4 folders 4-13
Series XI. Oral History Interviews. Personal Box 4 folders 11
Series XII. Photographs Box 4 folders 12-14
Series XIII. Subject Files Box 4 folders 16-31
Cite As:
Paul Jones Chapman papers, #22-1-2102. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Scope and content
Includes correspondence concerning the graduate program of the New York State Agricultural Experiment Station in Geneva, 1940-1983; selection of a head for the Entomology Department at Cornell University, 1965; and Rachel Carson's SILENT SPRING, 1962-1963. Also, research material concerning SILENT SPRING, the experiment stations, grant files, annual review articles, and photographs.
Photographs. Entomology -- Study and teaching. Carson, Rachel, 1907-1964. (Title of work: Silent spring..) New York State College of Agriculture. Department of Entomology Cornell University -- : Faculty. Cornell University -- : Graduate students. New York State Agricultural Experiment Station
